title: Food and Drinks in English
description: Order your favorites anywhere in the world!

scenes.speech: Food English works everywhere in the world! 🍔
scenes.title: Food in English
scenes.content: This lesson is about food and drinks in English. Build the power to order your favorites in restaurants while traveling or studying abroad.

scenes.speech: Can you say your favorite food in English?
scenes.title: Food Words
scenes.content: pizza, hamburger, sushi, ramen, sandwich, salad, rice, noodles. Starting with what you love is the trick to keep going.
scenes.highlight:
🍕 pizza  🍔 burger  🍣 sushi
🍜 ramen  🥗 salad  🍚 rice

scenes.title: Drinks
scenes.content: water, milk, juice, tea, coffee, soda. Remember 'water' for the most essential one. 'Can I have some water?' is super handy.
scenes.highlight:
💧 water  🥛 milk  🧃 juice
🍵 tea  ☕ coffee  🥤 soda

scenes.speech: 'I'm hungry' and 'I'm thirsty' kick off conversations!
scenes.title: Saying How You Feel
scenes.content: I'm hungry. I'm thirsty. Two simple lines to start a meal conversation at home or out.

scenes.title: Ordering
scenes.content: Can I have a hamburger, please? The polite request pattern. Adding 'please' immediately raises the impression.
scenes.highlight:
Can I have ___, please?
I'd like ___, please.

scenes.speech: Tell what you like and dislike too!
scenes.title: Likes and Dislikes
scenes.content: I like sushi. I don't like onions. If you have allergies, learn 'I'm allergic to peanuts.' too.
scenes.highlight:
I like ___ .
I don't like ___ .
I'm allergic to ___ .

scenes.speech: Share how it tastes in English!
scenes.title: Reactions
scenes.content: It's delicious! Yummy! Tasty! Saying the meal is good makes the cook happy.

scenes.speech: Asking for the check also matters!
scenes.title: The Check
scenes.content: Check, please. Or Can I have the bill, please? Both are essential phrases at the end of a meal.
scenes.highlight:
Check, please.
Can I have the bill?

scenes.speech: End on a 'thank you' for warmth!
scenes.title: Thank you!
scenes.content: Thank you for the meal. The English version of expressing gratitude after eating. Saying it warms up the table.

scenes.speech: Quiz! How do you politely ask for water?
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the most polite way to ask for water?
quizChoices: Can I have some water, please?
scenes.quizExplanation: Can I have some water, please? is polite and natural. Don't forget 'please'.

scenes.speech: You'll never starve in any country now! 🌟
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
